headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: BlackRock's voting rights in XPS Pensions Group fall to 9.94% from 10.74% following client instruction on voting rights delegation.
summary: BlackRock, Inc. notified a decrease in its holding in XPS Pensions Group PLC (ISIN: GB00BDDN1T20) on 17 July 2026, with the threshold crossed on 16 July. Total voting rights fell from 10.74% to 9.94%, comprising 9.07% from direct shares (18.9m voting rights) and 0.87% from financial instruments (securities lending and CFDs). The reduction resulted from a client instruction to elect for decision-making on voting rights, effectively delegating voting control.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholding change below the 10% threshold; indicates passive management delegation rather than strategic repositioning, likely immaterial to equity investors.

headline: Notice of AGM
brief: Firering Strategic Minerals announces AGM scheduled for 12 August 2026 in London.
summary: Firering Strategic Minerals plc (AIM: FRG) has set its Annual General Meeting for 12 August 2026 at 10:00am BST at Hill Dickinson LLP in London. The lime producer and critical minerals explorer increased its Limeco stake to 45% in June 2026. Limeco, a Zambian quicklime project that began production in early 2025, targets 600-800 tonnes daily output to supply Central African copper producers. The company is also advancing the Atex lithium-tantalum project in Côte d'Ivoire.
retail_take: Routine governance notice with underlying operational context; no immediate market impact. AGM disclosure is standard procedure; focus remains on Limeco's production ramp-up execution.

headline: Transfer of Treasury Shares
brief: BTG Consulting transfers 31,313 treasury shares to settle partner staff share option exercises.
summary: BTG Consulting PLC transferred 31,313 ordinary shares (5p each) from treasury on 20 July 2026 to settle exercised partner staff share options. The company now holds 112,965 treasury shares remaining. Total shares in issue stand at 161,487,641 with 161,374,676 voting rights (excluding treasury shares). This is routine share option settlement activity.
retail_take: Routine administrative disclosure for employee share option settlement; no material implications for valuation or capital structure. Standard corporate governance practice.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Pehlwan Malik Holdings Limited crosses 3% voting rights threshold in CT Automotive Group PLC.
summary: Pehlwan Malik Holdings Limited, controlled by Jameel Malik, acquired voting rights in CT Automotive Group PLC (ISIN GB00BMHYGR77), crossing the 3% disclosure threshold on 15 July 2026. The entity now holds 2,213,089 voting rights representing 3.0% of the company's voting rights, acquired through direct shareholding with no financial instruments involved. The issuer was notified on the same day, with completion recorded in the United Arab Emirates on 17 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine major shareholding disclosure showing a new 3% stake; no indication of strategic intent, activist involvement, or material corporate development. Standard regulatory notification with no apparent market implications.

headline: Aktietilbagekøb i AL Sydbank A/S – transaktioner i uge 29
AL Sydbank A/S share buyback programme: transactions in week 29
brief: AL Sydbank repurchased 30,000 shares in week 29 at average price DKK 607.07, bringing total buyback to 917,000 shares.
summary: AL Sydbank executed its DKK 1.1bn share buyback programme announced 25 February 2026, commencing 2 March and due to complete 31 January 2027. In week 29 (13-17 July), the bank purchased 30,000 shares via Danske Bank at average price DKK 607.07, totalling DKK 18.2m. Cumulative programme spending stands at DKK 490.8m for 917,000 shares. The bank now holds 918,569 treasury shares representing 1.04% of capital.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within disclosed parameters; buyback on track at approximately 83% programme completion by mid-July with six months remaining. No material implications for investors.

headline: DRIS Issue Price
brief: Hargreave Hale AIM VCT 1 sets DRIS reference price at 31.69p for interim dividend reinvestment.
summary: Hargreave Hale AIM VCT 1 PLC has set the reference price for its Dividend Re-investment Scheme (DRIS) at 31.69p per Ordinary Share, based on the last published ex-dividend NAV as of 17 July 2026. The interim dividend was announced on 17 June 2026. New shares issued under the DRIS will have a payment and allotment date of 31 July 2026, with admission and dealing expected around 7 August 2026.
retail_take: Routine administrative announcement establishing the reinvestment price for dividend holders; no material implications beyond mechanical execution of existing dividend policy.

headline: Ferguson Announces Completion of Cancellation of Secondary Listing on London Stock Exchange
Acquisition of Italian Biogas Plant
brief: Zenith Energy to acquire Italian biogas plant for €1.6m cap, targeting €2.5m annual EBITDA with government support.
summary: Zenith Energy entered binding agreement to acquire 100% of Italian biogas developer with fully permitted, construction-ready facility producing ~3m cubic metres methane annually. Project economics show €5m projected revenue, €2.5m EBITDA (50% margin), supported by 15-year government incentives and long-term feedstock supply contract. Purchase capped at €1.6m; green project financing targeted Q3 2026, acquisition completion Q4 2026, operations expected Q3 2027. Italy's PNIEC targets 5.7bn cubic metres biomethane by 2030, creating portfolio expansion opportunity alongside existing solar business.
retail_take: Market likely positive: acquisition adds high-margin recurring revenue stream with government-backed incentives, leverages Zenith's Italian operational experience since 2013, funded non-dilutively. Low execution risk given fully permitted status and secured feedstock.

headline: Arbitration Update
brief: Panthera's Indian subsidiary files reply in US$1.58bn arbitration claim against India over breach of bilateral investment treaty.
summary: Indo Gold Pty Ltd (IGPL), Panthera Resources' Australian subsidiary, filed its Reply on Merits and Counter-Memorial on 17 July 2026 in a US$1.58bn arbitration claim against India. The claim concerns alleged breaches of the 1999 Australia-India bilateral investment treaty over the Bhukia gold project in Rajasthan, specifically the denial of prospecting licence rights and India's 2021 legislative changes eliminating preferential mining rights. Phase One hearings are scheduled for 14-19 December 2026 at The Hague, with India's rejoinder due 23 October 2026. Damages quantum remains reserved for Phase Two.
retail_take: Procedural progress on a high-value but highly uncertain claim; arbitration moving to hearing stage reduces near-term development optionality but outcome remains unpredictable with material enforcement risks.

headline: Purchase of own shares
brief: OTE completes share buyback tranche, purchasing 125,505 shares for €2.49m at average price of €19.80.
summary: Hellenic Telecommunications Organization S.A purchased 125,505 own shares between 10-17 July 2026 as part of its 2026 buyback programme, spending €2.49 million at an average price of €19.80 per share. Following the purchase, OTE holds 1,377,529 treasury shares representing 0.350% of outstanding shares. The buyback was executed under EU market abuse regulations.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically signal management confidence but this modest tranche size and regular execution suggest standard treasury optimization rather than material market signal.

headline: AIB Group - Transaction in Own Shares
brief: AIB repurchased 2.5m shares at €10.23-€10.56 in July as part of €1bn buyback programme.
summary: AIB Group purchased 2,498,500 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 on Euronext Dublin via Goodbody Stockbrokers at prices ranging from €10.23 to €10.56 per share, with volume-weighted averages between €10.31-€10.51 daily. These repurchased shares will be cancelled, reducing total shares in issue to 2,088,280,504. The purchases form part of the company's €1 billion share buyback programme announced 4 March 2026.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically support EPS and signal management confidence in valuation, though represent capital allocation choice rather than operational improvement. Market neutral to modestly positive signal.

headline: Share repurchase programme: Transactions of week 29 2026
brief: Jyske Bank repurchased 59,112 shares in week 29 2026, bringing total treasury holdings to 1.53m shares (2.63% of capital).
summary: Jyske Bank completed share repurchases during 13-17 July 2026 under its DKK 3bn programme (running through January 2027). The bank acquired 59,112 shares at average prices ranging from DKK 976.48 to DKK 996.59, spending approximately DKK 59.5m. Accumulated repurchases total 1,528,781 shares (2.63% of share capital) at an average price of DKK 915.28 per share, representing DKK 1.399bn spent to date.
retail_take: Routine capital management disclosure; buyback progresses modestly within announced parameters at relatively steady prices, indicating steady execution rather than opportunistic buying—neutral signal for investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Trustpilot repurchased 354,780 shares (13-17 July) at average 268.89p-283.37p per day for cancellation.
summary: Trustpilot Group PLC bought 354,780 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 via Deutsche Bank at prices ranging 250.60p–285.00p per share. Total cost excluding fees: ~£950,000. Since programme launch 25 March 2026, the company has purchased 3,479,756 shares for ~£8.6m, all designated for cancellation. Trading executed on LSE under the previously announced buyback programme.
retail_take: Routine capital management exercise; on-programme share cancellation is mechanically EPS-accretive and signals management confidence in valuation, but signals no material news.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Balfour Beatty repurchased 457,778 shares (13-17 July) as part of ongoing buyback programme; 14.2m shares bought to date at 801.30p average.
summary: Balfour Beatty purchased 457,778 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 via Merrill Lynch International under its share buyback programme announced January 2026 and increased March 2026. Cumulative repurchase under the buyback stands at 14,212,629 shares at volume-weighted average price of 801.2974p, held in treasury with no voting rights. Remaining voting shares total 478,725,518. Prices ranged 831-871p across LSE and alternative venues during the buyback period.
retail_take: Routine capital allocation disclosure; share buybacks typically indicate management confidence in valuation but signal no major M&A activity. Non-event for most investors unless buyback pace accelerates materially.

headline: Publication of Scheme Document
brief: H.B. Fuller to acquire Advanced Medical Solutions in recommended cash deal via scheme of arrangement.
summary: Advanced Medical Solutions (AMS) has published its scheme document for a recommended cash acquisition by H.B. Fuller Medical Adhesive Technologies Inc., a wholly-owned H.B. Fuller subsidiary. Court and General Meetings scheduled for 12 August 2026 in London. Deal requires approval by 75% in value of scheme shareholders at Court Meeting and special resolutions at General Meeting. AMS Directors unanimously recommend approval, with directors holding ~745,766 shares (0.34% of issued capital) committing support. Expected completion Q3/Q4 2026 subject to regulatory approvals. AMS will delist from AIM and re-register as private company post-completion.
retail_take: Acquisition announcement already known from June; this is procedural scheme document publication ahead of shareholder votes. Market should view positively given board unanimity and adviser support, though deal completion remains conditional on regulatory clearance through Q3/Q4.

headline: Jadestone Energy PLC - Holding(s) in Company
brief: Artemis Investment Management LLP crosses 4% shareholding threshold in Jadestone Energy PLC via acquisition.
summary: Artemis Investment Management LLP notified on 17 July 2026 that it crossed a major holding threshold in Jadestone Energy PLC on 16 July 2026. The fund manager now holds 21,851,288 shares representing 4.009932% of shares outstanding, up from a previous position of 3.123301%. The holding is direct equity only with no financial instruments involved.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding crossing regulatory threshold; no material implications for retail investors unless part of broader strategic positioning by Artemis.

headline: Sulnox Recognised in Superyacht UK Awards
brief: Sulnox's fuel conditioning technology Sulnox Eco wins Superyacht UK sustainability award via distribution partner Barrow International.
summary: Sulnox Eco has been recognised for the 2026 Superyacht UK Sector Sustainability Award, with the accolade awarded to distribution partner Barrow International. The technology delivers ~5% fuel consumption reduction, ~90% particulate emissions reduction, and 28% HVO lubricity improvement. The global superyacht fleet exceeds 6,000 vessels; sector emissions projected to reach 10 million tonnes CO2 annually by 2030. Barrow's submission progresses to British Marine Sector Awards National Finals in September 2026.
retail_take: Industry recognition validates Sulnox Eco's commercial value in high-margin superyacht sector with tangible environmental and cost benefits, supporting market positioning and potential customer acquisition in premium segment.

headline: Transaction in own Shares and Total Voting Rights
brief: LSL Property Services repurchased 150,000 shares at 227-230p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: LSL Property Services purchased 150,000 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 via Shore Capital at volume weighted average prices of 227.4p (32,400 shares on 14 July) and 230.4p (117,600 shares on 16 July). The buyback forms part of the programme announced 27 January 2026. Treasury holdings now total 5,764,809 shares, with 99,394,141 shares in issue excluding treasury stock.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within announced programme; signals management confidence in valuation but no material market catalyst; standard capital allocation activity.

headline: Appointment of Chief Financial Officer
brief: Concurrent Technologies appoints Emma Ciechan as CFO from 5 October 2026, replacing retiring Kim Garrod.
summary: Emma Ciechan, aged 50, joins as CFO and Executive Director effective 5 October 2026, bringing 20+ years' leadership experience in strategic finance, capital allocation and corporate transactions. She joins from DS Smith (£6.8bn revenue), where she was Finance Director of major divisions, leading teams of up to 90 people. Outgoing CFO Kim Garrod, who announced her retirement in April 2026, will remain employed until 30 November 2026 to facilitate transition.
retail_take: Positive read: experienced hire from large international manufacturing background strengthens finance function as company executes growth strategy; orderly transition with overlap period mitigates execution risk.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Saga CEO and CFO purchase shares under employee Share Incentive Plan on 15 July 2026.
summary: Mike Hazell (Group Chief Executive Officer) and Mark Watkins (Group Chief Financial Officer) each purchased 276 ordinary shares in Saga plc at £6.5198 per share on 15 July 2026, totalling £1,799.47 each. The purchases were made under the Saga plc Share Incentive Plan annual participation scheme. This represents an initial notification of director shareholding changes under Market Abuse Regulation Article 19.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of employee share scheme participation by senior management; modest purchase amounts suggest standard annual SIP participation rather than significant conviction buying.

headline: Exercise of Options, PDMR Shareholding & TVR
brief: Intercede CFO Nitil Patel exercises 500,000 share options at 38p; holding rises to 0.92% of voting rights.
summary: CFO Nitil Patel exercised 500,000 share options under the Enterprise Management Incentive Plan at 38p per share on 20 July 2026. Following exercise, Patel holds 560,097 ordinary shares (0.92% of total voting rights). The 500,000 new shares are admitted to AIM trading on or around 24 July 2026. Post-admission, the company will have 61,036,452 ordinary shares in issue with 60,662,546 total voting rights.
retail_take: Routine disclosure showing management confidence through option exercise; no material impact indicated. Standard PDMR shareholding notification with no strategic significance for investors.

headline: Appointment of Non-Executive Director
brief: Accesso appoints Philip Wood as Non-Executive Director; former Aptitude Software CFO to chair Audit Committee.
summary: Accesso Technology Group PLC has appointed Philip Wood, aged 53, as Non-Executive Director effective 20 July 2026. Wood brings 25+ years as finance leader in listed software, serving as CFO and Deputy CEO of Aptitude Software Group plc until July 2023, and previously as Chair of Audit Committee at SmartSpace Software plc (2021-2024). He will join the Audit Committee and succeed Andy Malpass as Audit Chair, bringing experience in SaaS transitions, financial governance, and public market navigation.
retail_take: Positive signal: appointment of experienced software sector CFO with audit governance expertise strengthens board credibility as Accesso pursues AI and data strategy expansion in competitive leisure-tech market.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Autotrader directors exercise share options and sell portions to cover tax; routine PDMR disclosure.
summary: Directors Nathan Coe and James Warner exercised nil-cost options on 17 July 2026 under the Performance Share Plan and Deferred Annual Bonus Plan. Coe exercised 148,898 shares, selling 70,158 at £4.97 (£348,457 proceeds) and retaining 78,740. Warner exercised 68,934 shares, selling 32,481 at £4.97 (£161,325 proceeds) and retaining 36,453. Sales covered tax and other deductions from vested equity incentives.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of vesting and tax-driven sales; directors retaining significant portions indicates confidence. No material market implication beyond standard PDMR notification requirement.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Telecom Plus non-exec director Carla Stent acquires 2,833 shares at £8.80 on 17 July.
summary: Carla Stent, Non-executive Director of Telecom Plus PLC, acquired 2,833 ordinary shares (5p each) on 17 July 2026 at £8.80 per share via the London Stock Exchange, totalling approximately £24,930. The transaction was split across two trades: 2,415 shares and 418 shares. The acquisition is disclosed under Article 19 of the EU Market Abuse Regulation as required for director shareholdings.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of director share purchase; insider buying can signal confidence but purchase size is modest and requires disclosure context rather than indicating material conviction.

headline: Directorate Changes
brief: Emma Lloyd appointed independent Non-Executive Director from 1 October 2026; Sarah Warby retires after eight years.
summary: Mony Group PLC announces planned board succession with Emma Lloyd joining as independent Non-Executive Director effective 1 October 2026, joining the Audit, Remuneration, Nomination and Risk & Sustainability Committees. Lloyd currently serves as Vice President, Partnerships EMEA at Netflix and previously held Chief Business Officer role at Sky Group, with nine years' non-executive experience at Ocado Plc. Sarah Warby steps down 2 October 2026 after eight years, with Chair Jonathan Bewes praising her contributions to the company's development.
retail_take: Routine board succession with experienced external hire; strengthens governance and digital expertise. Market likely views this as orderly planning with no material concerns.

headline: CEO Succession
brief: DP Poland appoints Łukasz Ostrowski as CEO from 3 August, replacing departing Nils Gornall after four-year tenure.
summary: Nils Gornall steps down as CEO and director effective 3 August 2026 after four years, during which DP Poland expanded from 116 to over 200 stores and shifted to a profitable franchise model. Incoming CEO Łukasz Ostrowski joins from OTCF S.A. (Deputy CEO) and brings extensive experience scaling multi-site consumer businesses and e-commerce platforms from senior roles at IKEA, Groupon Poland, and other major retailers. Gornall's unvested share options will fully vest upon departure, totalling 15,560,075 options. Trading remains in line with Board expectations; FY2026 outlook unchanged.
retail_take: Successor has strong pedigree in scaling retail and e-commerce operations across Poland and internationally, directly relevant to Domino's digital-led model, with smooth transition planned. Market likely views this positively given track record and business momentum under outgoing CEO.

headline: Admission and Commencement of Dealings
brief: Technology Minerals PLC admits to LSE Main Market; 12.5bn ordinary shares now trading.
summary: Technology Minerals PLC (LSE: TM1) commenced trading on the London Stock Exchange Main Market effective 20 July 2026 under the Equity Shares (Transition) category. The company has 12,495,477,697 ordinary shares of £0.0005 each in issue with no treasury holdings. Technology Minerals focuses on building a circular economy for battery metals through recycling, recovery and re-use of Li-ion batteries, aligned with the UK Government's Critical Minerals Strategy and national resilience objectives.
retail_take: IPO commencement is positive sentiment, signalling market confidence in the battery metals/critical minerals thesis and UK government-aligned strategy, though broader market reception depends on valuation and trading momentum.

headline: Director's Dealing
brief: Javier del Ser Pérez's family purchases 122,000 Steppe Cement shares at 21.5p, raising stake to 8.96%.
summary: The family of Executive Chairman Javier del Ser Pérez acquired 122,000 ordinary shares in Steppe Cement Limited at 21.5 pence per share on 17 July 2026 via Portola Group Limited. This purchase increases their total holding to 19,625,115 shares, representing approximately 8.96% of issued share capital. The transaction was conducted on the London Stock Exchange AIM Market.
retail_take: Insider buying by the Executive Chairman's family signals confidence in the company but remains a routine disclosure with modest stake increase and no material corporate implications.

headline: Completion of Dosing in Toxicology Programme
brief: Sareum completes dosing in Phase 2-enabling toxicology programme for lead autoimmune drug SDC-1801, on schedule for year-end regulatory package completion.
summary: Cambridge-based biotech Sareum Holdings (AIM: SAR) has completed dosing in the Phase 2-enabling toxicology programme for SDC-1801, a selective oral TYK2/JAK1 inhibitor targeting autoimmune diseases including psoriasis. The programme, restarted in February 2026, finished on schedule using existing cash resources. The company is now conducting data analysis and CMC/formulation development, targeting completion of the full Phase 2-enabling regulatory package by Q4 2026, building on encouraging Phase 1 results showing once-daily dosing potential with no safety concerns.
retail_take: Positive progression milestone for clinical-stage biotech on track with timelines and cash runway; demonstrates execution capability and advances lead candidate toward Phase 2, reducing near-term binary risk.

headline: Withdrawal of AGM Resolutions 10 and 11
brief: Inspiration Healthcare withdraws two AGM resolutions on pre-emption rights and share buyback authority following shareholder feedback.
summary: Inspiration Healthcare Group PLC has withdrawn Resolutions 10 and 11 from its AGM scheduled for 23 July 2026. These special resolutions concerned disapplication of pre-emption rights for equity allotments and authority to repurchase own shares. The Board withdrew them following feedback from a significant shareholder, though believes the authorities serve shareholders' interests. The company states it does not currently anticipate needing these powers before the next AGM and would seek approval separately if required.
retail_take: Non-event for markets; company retains operational flexibility and can request shareholder approval later if needed, suggesting no immediate capital plans constrained by the withdrawal.

headline: EBT Share Purchase
brief: Ultimate Products' EBT purchased 32,500 shares at 48.47p, holding 4.1% of issued capital.
summary: On 17 July 2026, JTC Employer Solutions Trustee Limited, acting as trustee of Ultimate Products' Employee Benefit Trust, purchased 32,500 ordinary shares at 48.47 pence per share. Following this transaction, the EBT holds 3,573,481 shares, representing approximately 4.1% of the Company's issued share capital. The EBT holds shares for employee benefit and vesting of awards under the Company's share incentive schemes.
retail_take: Routine administrative disclosure reflecting standard employee share scheme activity; no material implications for equity valuation or strategic direction.

headline: Quarterly Report: June 2026
brief: South32 sells aluminium assets to Alcoa for US$5.6B, pivots to base metals focus with strong FY26 production results.
summary: South32 announced sale of aluminium value chain (excluding Mozal) to Alcoa for up to US$5.6B enterprise value plus US$1.2B rehabilitation provisions, completing H2 FY27. FY26 exceeded production guidance: Sierra Gorda +2%, Cannington +2%, with 15% Q4 sales volume increase. Sierra Gorda fourth grinding line approved, targeting 30% copper equivalent production increase from FY31. Hermosa Taylor project received final permitting; US$710M invested FY26. Post-transaction, 85% pro-forma earnings from base/precious metals; 55% production growth from approved projects.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively: transformational portfolio shift unlocks shareholder value, strong operational delivery, major projects approved. Execution risk on Alcoa deal completion and FY27 guidance revision warrant monitoring.

headline: Helium Sales Completed
brief: Helix Exploration completes first helium sales from Rudyard facility, 24 months post-IPO, with 160 Mcf jumbo trailer departed.
summary: Helix Exploration (AIM: HEX) has shipped its first commercial helium cargo—a 160 Mcf compressed helium-loaded jumbo tube trailer from its Rudyard, Montana facility—to an industrial gases customer under a spot sales arrangement announced in May 2026. Commercial operations commenced just over two years after IPO. The customer is supplying additional trailers for continuous filling; Helix has leased a second trailer for rotation. Three wells are producing; a fourth (Inez-1) is pending re-entry. The Keyes Helium Complex acquisition enhances liquefaction optionality. Global helium supplies remain constrained post-Qatar production loss.
retail_take: Significant operational milestone demonstrating progression from exploration to revenue generation ahead of schedule, with supportive helium market fundamentals; likely positive read as company transitions to cash-generative phase.

headline: Statement regarding possible offer from AEW
US$750,000 Strategic Partnership Payment Received
brief: Kazera receives US$750k initial payment from South African mining partner SAI under long-term Heavy Mineral Sands cooperation agreement.
summary: Kazera Global received US$750,000 from South Africa AT Investments (Pty) Ltd, the first commercial milestone under a Mining Cooperation and Production Sharing Agreement with subsidiary Whale Head Minerals announced 9 July 2026. SAI will fund 100% of capital and operating costs for HMS operations at Walviskop and the 2A Project (subject to mining right approval), while WHM retains 20% production entitlement and ownership of mining rights. A further US$1.75 million payment is due upon 2A Mining Right approval. Combined with recent US$500,000 from Hebei Settlement, this strengthens Kazera's funding position and eliminates need for additional drawdowns on existing loan facilities.
retail_take: Positive signal of partnership viability and financial stability. De-risks near-term funding and validates HMS asset value through third-party investment commitment, though execution on Mine Plan and regulatory approvals remain critical milestones.

headline: SEALAND SECURES COMPUTING ADVISORY MANDATE
brief: Sealand Capital Galaxy secures computing advisory mandate with Hong Kong-listed ITC Properties Group, with revenue recognised this quarter.
summary: Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited (LSE: SCGL) has been appointed to provide advisory services to ITC Properties Group Limited (HKEX: 00199), a Hong Kong Stock Exchange-listed company. The mandate covers enterprise computing infrastructure planning, digital transformation initiatives, and governance frameworks, with services delivered during July 2026. Revenue will be recognised in Q2 2026. Parties intend to explore further collaboration opportunities in data security advisory and technology ecosystem development. The engagement represents commercialisation of Sealand's computing infrastructure expertise and complements its broader AI strategy across enterprise software, digital finance and energy technologies.
retail_take: Modest positive signal: validates Sealand's advisory capabilities and demonstrates commercial traction in computing infrastructure services, though mandate scale and financial materiality remain unclear from announcement.

headline: Appointment of joint corporate broker
brief: Frasers Group appoints Panmure Liberum as joint corporate broker alongside existing advisers.
summary: Frasers Group PLC has appointed Panmure Liberum Limited as Joint Corporate Broker with immediate effect as of 20 July 2026. The appointment expands the company's broker team to four members, joining existing brokers Barclays Bank plc, Deutsche Numis, and Jefferies. No financial terms or strategic rationale disclosed.
retail_take: Routine corporate housekeeping; expanding broker representation typically signals preparation for potential capital markets activity but provides no concrete catalyst.

headline: IPC Files Current Report on Form 8-K
brief: International Paper appoints two new independent directors to its board effective 1 October 2026.
summary: International Paper's Board of Directors appointed Katherine Collins and Lori J. Ryerkerk as non-employee independent directors, effective 1 October 2026, upon recommendation of the Governance Committee. The appointments were disclosed via Form 8-K filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on 20 July 2026. Further details are available on the company's website and SEC filings.
retail_take: Routine board governance update; director appointments typically signal normal succession planning and board refreshment with no immediate material implications for investors.

headline: New Copper-Silver-Gold  Project, Nevada
brief: Sunrise Resources secures Nevada copper-silver-gold project with historical drill intercepts grading 0.73% copper and 31g/t silver.
summary: Sunrise Resources has acquired the Lake Copper-Silver-Gold Project in Nevada's Humboldt Range through a lease-option agreement. Historical drilling by Utah International (BHP subsidiary) in 1985 intersected 50m grading 0.73% copper, 31g/t silver, and 0.2g/t gold from surface. Company surface sampling returned up to 198g/t silver, 0.13% copper and 0.3g/t gold. Terms include US$100 upfront, annual payments rising to US$20,000, 2% NSR royalty (buyable for US$500,000), and US$150,000 purchase option. Project targets intrusion-related copper-precious metals deposits with skarn mineralisation across 450m arsenic-antimony anomaly.
retail_take: Early-stage exploration play on copper (energy transition metal) in premier Nevada district; historically significant grades warrant geophysics and drilling, but early-stage risk and minimal capex commitments mean limited near-term catalysts for retail investors.

headline: Second Malaysian Campaign Well Flows at 2,800 bopd
brief: Second well in Jadestone's Malaysia drilling campaign flows at 2,800 bopd, setting new extended reach drilling record.
summary: Jadestone Energy's second well (EBA-07ST1) in its 2026 East Belumut campaign achieved 2,800 bopd production, drilled 13% under budget with a 930-metre horizontal section. The well set a new Malaysia extended reach drilling record with a 4.1 ERD ratio and 5,473-metre measured depth. Combined with the first well (3,200 bopd peak), the two wells have increased field production by over 150% (~6,000 bopd), validating the company's strategy of unlocking bypassed reserves. A third contingent well has completed drilling with results forthcoming.
retail_take: Strong operational execution and reserve upside from existing Malaysian asset likely viewed positively by markets; demonstrates technical capability and value creation from mature field optimization, supporting growth narrative.

headline: Subscription Agreement Update - £0.2m Proceeds
brief: Smarter Web Company places 703,160 shares for £207k under December 2025 subscription agreement.
summary: The Smarter Web Company PLC placed 703,160 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 generating gross proceeds of £207,227 (approximately £0.295 per share), with the company receiving 98.25% after expenses. This tranche represents partial fulfilment of a subscription agreement announced 24 December 2025, leaving 47.4 million shares still available to place. The company provides web design, development and marketing services, accepts Bitcoin payments, and maintains Bitcoin treasury holdings as part of its corporate strategy.
retail_take: Modest capital raise completing an existing placement facility; suggests steady but incremental fundraising rather than strategic catalyst. Market likely views this as routine execution given substantial remaining tranche availability.

headline: Change of auditor
brief: Digitalbox PLC replaces auditor HaysMac with Cooper Parry Group, effective FY2026, following tender process.
summary: Digitalbox plc has accepted the resignation of current auditor HaysMac LLP and identified Cooper Parry Group Limited as its preferred successor auditor for the financial year ending 31 December 2026. The change follows a tender review overseen by the audit committee. HaysMac confirmed no circumstances connected with its resignation require disclosure to members or creditors under Section 519 of the Companies Act 2006. Regulatory and independence checks are ongoing.
retail_take: Routine auditor rotation following competitive tender; non-event unless new auditor raises material concerns during transition procedures.

headline: Notice of Cyber Security Incident
brief: Craneware discloses cyber security incident involving unauthorised data access; operations unaffected.
summary: Craneware identified a cyber security incident involving unauthorised access to a subset of its data environment on 20 July 2026. External forensic specialists confirm the incident is contained with no residual compromise. A significant volume of file names were exfiltrated, primarily non-sensitive or already public regulatory data, though some employee data and customer/partner records were accessed. The company has notified UK Information Commissioner's Office and FBI; investigations ongoing to determine precise scope of affected data and required notifications.
retail_take: Investors likely to view negatively due to data breach exposure and regulatory obligations, though swift containment and no operational disruption may limit damage. Reputational and potential compliance costs remain uncertain.

headline: Bexsero 1-dose booster EMA file acceptance
brief: EMA accepts GSK's Bexsero application for single-dose MenB booster in 10+ year-olds, potentially expanding market.
summary: GSK announced EMA acceptance of a regulatory submission to add a single booster dose of Bexsero (4CMenB) for individuals aged 10+ previously vaccinated in infancy. The application is supported by Phase 3b data showing effective immune response boosting. Bexsero has distributed 138 million doses globally since 2015 and is included in 15 EU infant programmes and 3 adolescent programmes. Serogroup B causes 56% of IMD cases in under-65s across EU/EEA, with peak incidence in infants and 15-24 year-olds.
retail_take: Modest positive; label expansion broadens addressable market for an established vaccine franchise, though approval timing and uptake remain uncertain and competitive MenB/MenACWY landscape exists.

headline: Investment manager arrangements and RFP process
brief: Impax Environmental Markets terminates investment manager agreement; launches RFP process for replacement.
summary: Impax Environmental Markets PLC has served notice to terminate its investment management agreement with Impax Asset Management (AIFM) Limited, effective 31 July 2027 (12-month notice period), though the Board has requested early waiver consideration. The Board's Management Engagement Committee is launching a fair and transparent Request for Proposal process to identify a new investment manager or strategic alternative, considering stakeholder views and future investment strategy. Advisers will be appointed to support the RFP process.
retail_take: Signals potential strategic change and seeks new management direction; market reaction depends on whether changes are viewed as necessary restructuring or indicate underlying performance concerns.

headline: Notice of AGM
brief: AdvancedAdvT Limited schedules AGM for 12 August 2026 at Singer Capital Markets offices in London.
summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited (LSE: ADVT) will hold its Annual General Meeting on Wednesday 12 August 2026 at 10:30 am BST at Singer Capital Markets, 1 Bartholomew Lane, London. The AGM circular, Form of Direction and Form of Proxy are available on the company's website at www.advancedadvt.com. AdvT operates in software solutions, business transformation, healthcare compliance and human capital management, leveraging AI and data analytics.
retail_take: Routine disclosure; no read-through. Standard AGM notification with standard timing and documentation arrangements.

headline: Corporate & Financing workstreams Update
brief: Savannah Resources advances Barroso Lithium Project financing with project finance debt process progressing and dual stock market listing under review.
summary: Project Finance debt advancing with conditional offers expected by year-end following Phase 1 DFS showing US$913m post-tax NPV. Company negotiating second spodumene offtake partnership to complement existing AMG agreement; secured non-binding LOIs for by-product sales up to 865ktpa (600ktpa assumed in DFS). Discussions ongoing with KfW IPEX-Bank and Euler Hermes on German Government loan guarantee. Board assessing dual regulated market listing in 2027 targeting European and major mining equity markets to improve investor access and support financing.
retail_take: Multiple financing workstreams progressing smoothly with strong lender interest and robust project economics supporting construction phase; dual listing signals confidence and broadens capital access, likely reading positively by markets.

headline: Progress towards Mining at Northern Zone
brief: Oracle Power progressing permitting for Northern Zone gold mining in Western Australia; major reports nearing completion.
summary: Oracle Power is advancing the Native Vegetation Clearing Permit (NVCP) and Mine Development and Closure Proposal (MDCP) for its Northern Zone Gold Project near Kalgoorlie, Western Australia. Site clearance and NVCP applications are due for submission this month, with fauna and flora reports in final preparation. The MDCP is progressing with heritage clearance surveys completed, hydrogeology nearing completion pending flood modelling, and geotechnical laboratory results received. CEO Naheed Memon stated the team is moving closer to commencing mining operations, with final deliverables expected for submission.
retail_take: Positive signal of regulatory progress toward production; demonstrates tangible momentum on a near-term asset after recent permit grant, though actual mining remains months away pending approvals.

headline: Insig AI makes first digital asset investment
brief: Insig AI makes first digital asset investment in Belgium-based AI micropayments firm 4Mica with €300k commitment.
summary: Insig AI has invested €300,000 via convertible loan in ATM Labs B.V. (trading as 4Mica), a Belgium-based early-stage AI agent micropayments provider, with completion expected within 45 days. The zero-interest convertible loan matures 31 August 2027 and represents 2.94% fully diluted equity if converted. An additional €1m conditional investment is committed if 4Mica secures substantial fundraising within 16 months. CEO Richard Bernstein has indicated willingness to underwrite further investment if required. 4Mica develops non-custodial clearing technology for stablecoin-based payments, claiming 99% cost reduction versus current transactions.
retail_take: Signals strategic pivot into digital assets post-100 opportunity review, though early-stage venture investment carries execution risk and represents modest initial capital deployment relative to institutional access claims.

headline: Kepler Trust Intelligence: New Research
brief: Kepler Trust Intelligence publishes new research on Fidelity Emerging Markets, highlighting its differentiated strategy and strong 2026 performance.
summary: Kepler Partners has released investment-grade research on Fidelity Emerging Markets (FEML), a £576.6m trust managed by Nick Price and Chris Tennant. FEML outperformed the MSCI EM Index by nearly 18 percentage points year-to-date through June 2026, with key holdings in Asian tech (SK Square, Elite Material) and Latin American materials. The trust trades at a 5.7% discount with 0.83% ongoing charges, while maintaining 63% net gearing. Kepler notes the distinctive approach using derivatives and frontier market exposure positions FEML well for long-term EM growth opportunities, particularly in AI supply chains and copper demand.
retail_take: Marketing research highlighting strong recent performance and attractive valuation could support sentiment, though the 5.7% discount and geopolitical risks noted temper upside enthusiasm for new investors.

headline: Kepler Trust Intelligence: New Research
brief: Kepler Trust Intelligence publishes new research note on JPMorgan European Growth & Income trust.
summary: Kepler Partners has released investment-grade research on JPMorgan European Growth & Income (JEGI), a diversified European core equity trust with a 3.7% dividend yield. The trust delivered 80% NAV total return over five years versus 53% for the MSCI Europe ex UK benchmark (to June 2026). JEGI trades at a -1.2% discount with 4% net gearing and a 0.64% ongoing charge. The trust is positioned as the default rollover option for European Opportunities Trust shareholders, with JPMorgan covering transition costs.
retail_take: Non-event for broader market; routine analyst research publication. Positive backdrop for JEGI holders given strong outperformance track record and merger opportunity, but limited immediate catalyst for price movement.

headline: Notice of Results and Investor Presentation
brief: Nichols PLC to announce H1 2026 interim results on 29 July, with investor presentation on 30 July via Investor Meet Company platform.
summary: Nichols plc, the diversified soft drinks group, will release its interim results for the half year ended 30 June 2026 on Wednesday 29 July 2026. CEO Andrew Milne and CFO Matthew Rothwell will present live on Thursday 30 July 2026 at 1:00pm BST via the Investor Meet Company platform. The presentation is open to all shareholders, with pre-event question submission available until 9am on 29 July. Free registration is available at investormeetcompany.com.
retail_take: Routine earnings announcement notice with investor engagement opportunity; no material information disclosed. Market reaction will depend on H1 results substance presented on 29-30 July rather than this procedural notice.

headline: Synectics' Synergy Achieves CAPSS Certification
brief: Synectics' Synergy platform achieves UK Government CAPSS cyber security certification for critical infrastructure.
summary: Synectics plc announced its Synergy security and surveillance platform has achieved Cyber Assurance of Physical Security Systems (CAPSS) certification from the UK National Protective Security Authority. The certification verifies compliance with UK Government cyber security standards for physical security systems deployed across critical national infrastructure including utilities, transport, policing and government facilities. Synergy is now the only integrated security platform from a UK-headquartered provider certified to the latest CAPSS 2025 standard and gains inclusion in the NPSA Catalogue of Security Equipment, enhancing visibility to government and infrastructure operators.
retail_take: Positive catalyst: certification removes a significant procurement barrier for CNI contracts and validates Synectics' secure-by-design approach, likely supporting near-term revenue opportunities in regulated markets where cyber resilience is increasingly mandatory.

headline: Return of Capital Update
brief: Helical completes capital return via B Share Scheme; 9.72p per share redemption proceeding as planned.
summary: Helical has allotted 122.3m B Shares following shareholder approval on 16 July 2026. Share consolidation (105:1 ratio) and admission of new ordinary shares occurred today. B Shares will be redeemed tomorrow at 9.72 pence each, with proceeds distributed by 4 August 2026. Post-consolidation, the company will have 117.5m new ordinary shares issued (985,873 held in treasury), equating to 116.5m voting rights.
retail_take: Routine capital return executing as announced; £12m distribution progressing smoothly with no material complications or changes disclosed. Non-event for markets.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Telecom Plus repurchased 504,956 shares at 861-886p per share during 13-17 July 2026 buyback programme.
summary: Telecom Plus PLC purchased 504,956 ordinary shares through Peel Hunt LLP between 13-17 July 2026 at prices ranging from 861p to 886p, with a weighted average price of 874.09p. The shares are being held in treasury. Following the purchase, the company holds 2.75m treasury shares and has 81.24m shares in issue, with 78.49m shares used as the denominator for FCA disclosure calculations.
retail_take: Routine capital management; share buybacks typically indicate management confidence in valuation but have limited market impact. Non-event unless accompanied by broader strategic context.

headline: Blanket Mine Q2 2026 Production Update
brief: Blanket Mine Q2 production of 17,360oz, up 18% QoQ; reaffirms full-year 2026 guidance of 72,000-76,500oz.
summary: Caledonia Mining reported Q2 2026 gold production of 17,360 ounces from its Blanket Mine in Zimbabwe, representing 18% growth from Q1's 14,767 ounces. Average grade improved to 2.88g/t in Q2, with July tracking 3.05g/t, reflecting successful restoration of higher-grade mining access. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance of 72,000 to 76,500 ounces, with production expected to accelerate in H2 driven by improved grades, completion of the elution plant upgrade, and expanded 7-day working week operations.
retail_take: Positive momentum signal as operational improvements gain traction and production recovers toward guidance targets; near-term grades trending favorably supports confidence in H2 acceleration, though full-year guidance remains unchanged from initial guidance.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: CVS Group repurchased 188,945 shares at 1,228-1,295p during July 13-17 under its May buyback programme.
summary: CVS Group purchased 188,945 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 at prices ranging from 1,228p to 1,295p per share, with a volume-weighted average of 1,266.84p. All shares will be cancelled post-settlement. Since the buyback programme's commencement, CVS has repurchased 1,409,494 shares at a weighted average of 1,228.45p. Following cancellation, CVS will have 68,747,690 shares in issue with no treasury shares held.
retail_take: Share buyback signals management confidence in valuation and capital discipline; cancellation reduces share count, supporting future EPS accretion. Routine execution suggests steady capital allocation.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Glenveagh Properties repurchased 331,879 shares at €2.39-€2.44 per share during 13-17 July as part of its ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Glenveagh Properties plc purchased 331,879 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin between 13-17 July 2026 via broker J&E Davy at prices ranging from €2.3900 to €2.4350 per share. The shares will be cancelled following settlement. This forms part of the company's share buyback programme announced 15 January 2026. Post-cancellation, Glenveagh will have 507,035,488 ordinary shares in issue.
retail_take: Share buybacks reduce share count and can support earnings per share; execution at relatively tight price range (€2.39-€2.44) suggests disciplined capital allocation during normal trading conditions.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: 3i Group purchases 1.1m shares for cancellation at average price of £26.54 as part of £750m buyback programme.
summary: 3i Group plc executed share buybacks between 13–17 July 2026, purchasing 222,305 shares daily through Barclays Capital Securities at the London Stock Exchange. Daily volume-weighted average prices ranged from £26.14 to £26.84 per share. To date, the company has repurchased 14.74m shares for £342.3m (excluding fees/taxes) under the £750m programme announced 14 May 2026. Total voting rights now stand at 1.01bn shares; no treasury shares held.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically indicate management confidence in valuation and support earnings per share, though execution around £26–27 signals measured pricing discipline rather than aggressive accumulation.

headline: Purchases of Own Shares
brief: Frasers Group initiates £80m share buyback programme via Panmure Liberum for up to 10m shares.
summary: Frasers Group has engaged Panmure Liberum to conduct a share buyback programme authorised at its September 2025 AGM, which approved purchases of up to 67.5m shares. The new arrangement permits Panmure Liberum to purchase up to 10m ordinary shares at 10p each, with an aggregate price cap of £80m, between now and mid-September 2026. Purchased shares will be placed into treasury; the sole purpose is to reduce share capital.
retail_take: Capital return via buyback typically signals management confidence in valuation and commitment to shareholder returns. Market likely neutral-to-positive, though execution timing and market conditions will determine impact on EPS accretion.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: IG Design Group purchased 620,000 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 at prices ranging from 81p to 84p, with a volume-weighted average price of 82.57p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing issued share capital from approximately 96.2m to 95.6m shares. retail_take: Routine capital allocation; share buybacks typically seen as neutral to slightly positive, signalling management confidence but offering modest EPS accretion. No market-moving read-through expected.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

retail_take: Routine capital management activity; modest buyback size suggests ongoing shareholder returns but no strategic signal. Standard regulatory disclosure with minimal market read-through.

headline: Results of 2026 Annual General and Special Meeting
brief: Pulsar Helium shareholders approve all AGM resolutions with overwhelming majorities at July 2026 meeting.
summary: Pulsar Helium's 2026 AGM held July 17 saw overwhelming shareholder approval across all matters. Board fixed at six directors, all re-elected with 98.69-99.53% support. Davidson & Company reappointed as auditor (99.38% approval). Stock Option Plan renewal passed 86.04%, Equity Incentive Plan amendments approved 86.00%, and shareholders increased the fixed reserve pool from 7.4m to 18m shares for RSU/PSU/DSU awards to directors, officers, employees and consultants.
retail_take: Routine corporate governance approval with strong shareholder backing; enables expanded equity compensation. Non-event for market as standard AGM housekeeping with no material business developments disclosed.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

The shares will be cancelled, reducing the issued share count to 184,377,640 shares. This forms part of the buyback programme announced on 7 April 2026.

retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within previously announced programme; modest scale suggests measured capital allocation, neither materially positive nor negative for shareholders.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Plus500 purchased 64,791 ordinary shares on 17 July 2026 at prices ranging from 4,216p to 4,318p, with a volume-weighted average of 4,263.96p. The shares will be held in treasury. retail_take: Routine share buyback continuation under approved programme; modest purchase size signals ongoing capital management. No material news or earnings implications.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: MITIE repurchased 875,000 shares (13-17 July) at 148.8-151.1p per share under its buyback programme.
summary: MITIE Group purchased 875,000 ordinary shares of 2.5p each over five trading days (13-17 July 2026) via Peel Hunt LLP at prices ranging from 148.80p to 151.10p, with a volume-weighted average price of 150.11p. retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks signal confidence in valuation but are non-event for investors absent broader strategic context or earnings implications.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Hunting PLC repurchased 61,500 ordinary shares at average 454.80p during 13-17 July 2026 under its buyback programme.
summary: Hunting PLC purchased 61,500 ordinary shares through Canaccord Genuity between 13-17 July 2026 at volume-weighted average price of 454.80p. Daily volumes ranged from 12,000-12,500 shares with prices between 447.50p and 462.75p. Following cancellation, issued share capital will reduce to 152,380,423 ordinary shares, with this figure used for disclosure threshold calculations under FCA rules.
retail_take: Routine execution of previously-announced buyback programme; capital allocation signal but insufficient scale to meaningfully impact earnings per share or market perception on its own.

headline: Update on the Litigation Process
brief: Tribunal rejects Morocco's request to separate jurisdictional objections from arbitration merits hearing.
summary: Emmerson's subsidiaries are pursuing ICSID arbitration against Morocco, claiming US$1.215bn in damages for alleged expropriation and treaty breaches. On 17 July 2026, the arbitration tribunal rejected Morocco's application to bifurcate proceedings, meaning jurisdictional objections will be addressed alongside the merits case rather than separately. The next procedural steps and hearing timeline remain to be confirmed. Emmerson emphasizes no certainty exists regarding the outcome of its treaty claims.
retail_take: Tribunal decision removes potential delay tactic but doesn't guarantee success; consolidated proceedings could accelerate resolution either way, though significant execution risk remains on a US$1.2bn claim.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: M.P. Evans cancels 2,355 shares purchased at average 1,636.68p on 17 July 2026.
summary: M.P. Evans Group PLC repurchased 2,355 of its own shares for cancellation on 17 July 2026 via broker Cavendish Capital Markets. Shares traded between 1,606.00p and 1,680.00p, with a volume-weighted average price of 1,636.68p. Following cancellation, the company has 52,159,904 shares in issue. All shares carry equal voting rights and none are held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback with modest scale; suggests modest capital allocation prioritising returns over growth; market likely views this as neutral maintenance activity.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pets at Home completed buyback of 903,553 shares at average 194.8p, reducing share count to 445.0m.
summary: Between 13–17 July 2026, Pets at Home repurchased 903,553 ordinary shares via Deutsche Bank at prices ranging 191.0p–199.6p (weighted average 194.8p). Shares will be cancelled. Post-transaction, 445,032,022 shares remain in issue with no treasury shares held. Total voting rights reset to 445,032,022 for FCA disclosure threshold calculations.
retail_take: Routine capital return; share buybacks are shareholder-friendly but typically market-neutral unless signalling undervaluation. No material operational news here—a mechanical treasury operation under existing programme.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: AEP Plantations purchased 57,142 ordinary shares on 17 July 2026 at prices ranging from 162.0p to 170.0p, with volume-weighted average price of 165.6p. Since the buyback programme announcement on 6 July, the company has acquired 477,173 shares in aggregate held as treasury stock. Following purchases, voting rights total 383,116,307, with 16,646,413 shares held in treasury attracting no voting rights.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity. Share buyback programmes are typically neutral; execution within stated price range indicates orderly market conditions with no obvious directional signal.

headline: Half Year 2026 Trading Update
brief: AMS reports H1 2026 revenues of £115.2m, up 3.9% YoY, maintaining full-year EBITDA guidance despite timing headwinds.
summary: Advanced Medical Solutions Group plc posted H1 2026 revenues of approximately £115.2 million versus £110.8 million in H1 2025, representing 3.9% growth. Performance was impacted by delayed orders shipping in early July and de-stocking from the company's European direct sales expansion strategy. The group, which acquired Peters Surgical and Syntacoll, states integration remains on track and the Board remains confident in delivering full-year 2026 EBITDA in line with market expectations.
retail_take: Modest revenue growth with headwinds offset by strategic expansion execution and maintained guidance suggests controlled momentum; likely viewed as steady progress without surprise upside.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Kerry Group repurchased 59,133 shares for cancellation between 13-17 July 2026 at €82.25-€83.75 per share.
summary: Kerry Group purchased 59,133 of its own A ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin from 13-17 July 2026 at prices ranging from €82.25 to €83.75 per share. The shares will be cancelled. This forms part of Kerry's €300 million buyback programme announced 17 February 2026. Following settlement and cancellation, Kerry will have 158,734,384 ordinary shares in issue.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and provide shareholder value through capital returns, though execution against a large programme may face market headwinds.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Vertu Motors repurchased 193,022 shares for cancellation under its ongoing buyback programme, with purchases between 73.4p and 76.1p.
summary: Vertu Motors purchased 193,022 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 at prices ranging from 73.4p to 76.05p, bringing total shareholder returns since July 2017 to £47.6m and reducing shares in issue by 21.7%. Following cancellation, the company will have 310,896,787 shares with voting rights. The purchases were executed through Shore Capital Stockbrokers under the buyback programme announced 5 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital allocation update demonstrating continued shareholder return commitment; the consistent execution at modest share prices suggests orderly buyback progression with no obvious valuation concern.

headline: Acquisition of eMARx
brief: MedPal AI acquires eMARx, a care home medication management software platform, completing its integrated Health OS.
summary: MedPal AI acquired Solid State Technologies Ltd (trading as eMARx) for approximately £474,000 total consideration (£133,066 cash plus 7,272,834 shares). eMARx generated £739,231 revenue and £106,273 profit for year ended March 2026, with 82% gross margin. The acquisition combines MedPal's robotic pharmacy dispensing hub with eMARx's eMAR software for care homes, creating an integrated platform spanning prescription, dispensing, delivery and bedside administration across the UK's 16,500 care homes market.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively: strategic vertical integration into growing care home medicines market with immediate £27bn addressable opportunity, established recurring software revenues, and cross-sell potential leveraging existing Care UK relationship, though share dilution and modest target size warrant monitoring.

headline: WRAP Retail Offer
brief: Technology Minerals launches retail share offer at £0.0005 per share following £2m institutional placing.
summary: Technology Minerals PLC is conducting a WRAP retail offer via Winterflood, allowing existing UK shareholders to subscribe for new ordinary shares at £0.0005 each—the same price as the £2m institutional placing completed on 17 July 2026. Proceeds will fund mineral exploration, battery metals opportunities, and working capital. The offer closes 23 July 2026 with a £100 minimum investment per participant. The company is repositioning as a national resilience company focused on UK battery metal recycling and critical minerals supply.
retail_take: Democratized follow-on to institutional raise suggests management confidence, but tight timeline and existing shareholder restriction limits upside surprise; routine capital raise reflecting UK critical minerals strategy focus.

headline: MMCI OLSA update
brief: Celsius disputes foreclosure notices on 40% MMCI stake issued by new lender Equinaire following loan assignment.
summary: Indian firm Kiri Industries (via subsidiary Equinaire) acquired the OLSA loan from Philippine sovereign wealth fund MIC on 9 June 2026 to secure copper ore supply. On 17 July 2026, Equinaire issued notices of default, foreclosure proceedings, and public auction for Celsius' 40% interest in MMCI, claiming a relinquishment notice to Sodor Inc. constituted default. Celsius contests the default claim and Equinaire's legal capacity to foreclose, pledging full legal protection of its interests.
retail_take: Significant governance dispute over asset ownership creates material uncertainty for Celsius shareholders; legal escalation likely ahead with outcome determining 40% stake retention.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Molten Ventures repurchased 140,671 shares at average prices between 589p and 617p during the week of 13-17 July 2026.
summary: Molten Ventures plc executed its ongoing share repurchase programme authorised on 28 January 2026. Deutsche Numis purchased 140,671 ordinary shares across five trading days: 24,815 shares on 13 July at 608.27p VWAP; 25,856 on 14 July at 608.88p; 30,000 on 15 July at 617.04p; 30,000 on 16 July at 602.38p; and 30,000 on 17 July at 589.20p. Following purchases, issued share capital stands at 189,046,450 shares with 15,675,328 in treasury, reducing voting rights to 173,371,122.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution within disclosed programme; modest weekly volume suggests steady capital management rather than opportunistic buying, with prices trending lower late-week. Non-event for investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Hill & Smith bought back 40,346 shares at 2865p-3000p per share in week of 13-17 July, continuing £100m programme.
summary: Hill & Smith PLC repurchased 40,346 ordinary shares (25p each) during 13-17 July 2026 via Jefferies at prices ranging from 2865p to 3000p, with volume-weighted average prices between 2897p-2981p across venues. Total shares now in issue: 78,453,514. To date, 2,433,209 shares have been purchased under the £100m buyback programme announced August 2025. Purchases executed across LSE, Chi-X, BATE, Aquis, and Turquoise venues.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution; buyback on-track at modest pace within authorised programme. No market-material signal unless completion pace or pricing diverges meaningfully from plan.

headline: Interim Results
brief: Pulsar Group reports 10% revenue growth to £33m and 39% Adjusted EBITDA rise to £5.1m in H1 2026, with ARR up £1.2m constant currency.
summary: Pulsar Group (AIM: PULS), an audience intelligence SaaS provider, delivered H1 2026 revenue of £33.0m (up 10%) with 97% recurring, and Adjusted EBITDA of £5.1m (up 39%) with 15% margin. ARR grew £1.2m on constant currency to £67.2m at 31 May 2026, driven by enterprise demand, particularly in EMEA & North America (up £1.4m). The group completed £8.0m refinancing with HSBC, improved operating cash generation to £3.3m, and launched AI products including Lumina and Saga. Net debt stood at £6.0m; further cost reductions expected by FY26 year-end.
retail_take: Positive on margin expansion and enterprise momentum offsetting macro headwinds, but modest constant-currency ARR growth of £1.2m and mid-market weakness temper enthusiasm; market likely reads as solid execution with improving unit economics.

headline: Retirement Disposal Distribution Update
brief: RESI distributing Living REIT shares to shareholders; £45m cash proceeds to follow via B Share scheme.
summary: Residential Secure Income PLC has completed its Retirement Disposal, with 66.1m Living REIT ordinary shares (£62.3m value) admitted to LSE on 16 July 2026 and scheduled for in-specie distribution to shareholders on 23 July 2026 at exchange ratio of 0.3570 LIVE shares per RESI share. The transaction also generated £45m cash consideration, to be returned to shareholders via a B Share bonus issue and redemption scheme expected in Q3 2026, subject to retaining £3.9m for transaction costs and working capital through the company's managed wind-down.
retail_take: Orderly capital return to shareholders completes a key disposal milestone in RESI's wind-down phase; routine execution of pre-announced transaction with no obvious surprises.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Jet2 completes first tranche of share buyback, repurchasing 396,954 shares at average price of £14.89 between 13-17 July.
summary: Jet2 plc purchased 396,954 ordinary shares (1.25p each) from Jefferies International Limited during 13-17 July 2026 as the first tranche of a buyback programme announced 8 July. Shares acquired at volume-weighted average prices ranging from £14.51-£15.09 across five trading days. Following cancellation, 190,518,516 shares remain in issue. The buyback was authorised at the company's September 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Share buyback supports earnings per share accretion and signals management confidence in valuation. Execution at modest price variation (£14.35-£15.18 range) shows disciplined purchasing; neutral-to-mildly-positive signal for capital allocation.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
brief: Sosandar repurchased 500,000 shares at 11.5p under existing authority; now holds 30.6m shares in treasury.
summary: On 17 July 2026, Sosandar PLC purchased 500,000 ordinary shares (0.1p each) at 11.5p per share, representing 0.2% of issued capital, under shareholder authority granted 1 April 2026 to buy up to 22.4m shares. Total voting rights adjusted to 217.6m shares following treasury holdings of 30.6m shares. The women's fashion retailer, listed on AIM, operates direct-to-consumer via Sosandar.com and third-party partnerships including NEXT, M&S, and John Lewis.
